[]\n    92→\n    93→        p = urlsplit(self.url)\n    94→\n    95→        path = p.path\n    96→        if not path:\n    97→            path = \"/\"\n    98→\n    99→        url.append(path)\n 108→    @staticmethod\n   109→    def _encode_params(data):\n   110→        \"\"\"Encode parameters in a piece of data.\n   111→\n   112→        Will successfully encode parameters when passed as a dict or a list of\n   113→        2-tuples. Order is retained if data is a list of 2-tuples but arbitrary\n   114→        if parameters are supplied as a dict.\n   115→        \"\"\"\n   116→\n   117→        if isinstance(data, (str, bytes)):\n   118→            return data\n   119→        elif hasattr(data, \"read\"):\n   120→            return data\n   121→        elif hasattr(data, \"__iter__\"):\n   122→            result = []\n   123→            for k, vs in to_key_val_list(data):\n   124→                if isinstance(vs, basestring) or not hasattr(vs, \"__iter__\"):\n   125→                    vs = [vs]\n   126→                for v in vs:\n   127→                    if v is not None:\n   128→                        result.append(\n   129→                            (\n   130→                                k.encode(\"utf-8\") if isinstance(k, str) else k,\n   131→                                v.encode(\"utf-8\") if isinstance(v, str) else v,\n   132→                            )\n   133→                        )\n   134→            return urlencode(result, doseq=True)\n   135→        else:\n   136→            return data\n   137→\n   138→    @staticmethod\n   139→    def _encode_files(files, data):\n   140→        \"\"\"Build the body for a multipart/form-data request.\n   141→\n   142→        Will successfully encode files when passed as a dict or a list of\n   143→        tuples. Order is retained if data is a list of tuples but arbitrary\n   144→        if parameters are supplied as a dict.\n   145→        The tuples may be 2-tuples (filename, fileobj), 3-tuples (filename, fileobj, contentype)\n   146→        or 4-tuples (filename, fileobj, contentype, custom_headers).\n   147→        \"\"\"\n   148→        if not files:\n   149→            raise ValueError(\"Files must be provided.\")\n   150→        elif isinstance(data, basestring):\n   151→            raise ValueError(\"Data must not be a string.\")\n   152→\n   153→        new_fields = []\n   154→        fields = to_key_val_list(data or {})\n   155→        files = to_key_val_list(files or {})\n   156→\n   157→        for field, val in fields:\n   158→            if isinstance(val, basestring) or not hasattr(val, \"__iter__\"):\n   159→                val = [val]\n   160→            for v in val:\n   161→                if v is not None:\n   162→                    # Don't call str() on bytestrings: in Py3 it all goes wrong.\n   163→                    if not isinstance(v, bytes):\n   164→                        v = str(v)\n   165→\n   166→                    new_fields.append(\n   167→                        (\n   168→                            field.decode(\"utf-8\")\n   169→                            if isinstance(field, bytes)\n   170→                            else field,\n   171→                            v.encode(\"utf-8\") if isinstance(v, str) else v,\n   172→                        )\n   173→                    )\n   174→\n   175→        for k, v in files:\n   176→            # support for explicit filename\n   177→            ft = None\n   178→            fh = None\n   179→            if isinstance(v, (tuple, list)):\n   180→                if len(v) == 2:\n   181→                    fn, fp = v\n   182→                elif len(v) == 3:\n   183→                    fn, fp, ft = v\n   184→                else:\n   185→                    fn, fp, ft, fh = v\n   186→            else:\n   187→                fn = guess_filename(v) or k\n   188→                fp = v\n   189→\n   190→            if isinstance(fp, (str, bytes, bytearray)):\n   191→                fdata = fp\n   192→            elif hasattr(fp, \"read\"):\n   193→                fdata = fp.read()\n   194→            elif fp is None:\n   195→                continue\n   196→            else:\n   197→                fdata = fp\n   198→\n   199→            rf = RequestField(name=k, data=fdata, filename=fn, headers=fh)\n   200→            rf.make_multipart(content_type=ft)\n   201→            new_fields.append(rf)\n   202→\n   203→        body, content_type = encode_multipart_formdata(new_fields)\n   204→\n   205→        return body, content_type\n   206→\n   207→\n   208→class RequestHooksMixin:\n   209→    def register_hook(self, event, hook):\n   210→        \"\"\"Properly register a hook.\"\"\"\n   211→\n   212→        if event not in self.hooks:\n   213→            raise ValueError(f'Unsupported event specified, with event name \"{event}\"')\n   214→\n   215→        if isinstance(hook, Callable):\n   216→            self.hooks[event].append(hook)\n   217→        elif hasattr(hook, \"__iter__\"):\n   218→            self.hooks[event].extend(h for h in hook if isinstance(h, Callable))\n   219→\n   220→    def deregister_hook(self, event, hook):\n   221→        \"\"\"Deregister a previously registered hook.\n   222→        Returns True if the hook existed, False if not.\n   223→        \"\"\"\n   224→\n   225→        try:\n   226→            self.hooks[event].remove(hook)\n   227→            return True\n   228→        except ValueError:\n   229→            return False\n   230→\n   231→\n   232→class Request(RequestHooksMixin):\n   233→    \"\"\"A user-created :class:`Request <Request>` object.\n   234→\n   235→    Used to prepare a :class:`PreparedRequest <PreparedRequest>`, which is sent to the server.\n   236→\n   237→    :param method: HTTP method to use.\n   238→    :param url: URL to send.\n   239→    :param headers: dictionary of headers to send.\n   240→    :param files: dictionary of {filename: fileobject} files to 259→\n   260→    def __init__(\n   261→        self,\n   262→        method=None,\n   263→        url=None,\n   264→        headers=None,\n   265→        files=None,\n   266→        data=None,\n   267→        params=None,\n   268→        auth=None,\n   269→        cookies=None,\n   270→        hooks=None,\n   271→        json=None,\n   272→    ):\n   273→        # Default empty dicts for dict           self.register_hook(event=k, hook=v)\n   283→\n   284→        self.method = method\n   285→        self.url = url\n   286→        self.headers = headers\n   287→        self.files = files\n transmission and returns it.\"\"\"\n   299→        p = PreparedRequest()\n   300→        p.prepare(\n   301→            method=self.method,\n   302→            url=self.url,\n   303→ PreparedRequest(RequestEncodingMixin, RequestHooksMixin):\n   316→    \"\"\"The fully mutable :class:`PreparedRequest <PreparedRequest>` object,\n   317→    containing the exact bytes that will be sent to the server.\n   318→\n   319→    Instances are generated from a :class:`Request <Request>` object, and\n   320→    should not be instantiated manually; doing so may produce undesirable\n   321→    effects.\n   322→\n   323→    Usage::\n   324→\n   325→      >>> import requests\n   326→      >>> req = requests.Request('GET', 'https://httpbin.org/get')\n   327→      >>> r = req.prepare()\n   328→      >>> r\n   329→      <PreparedRequest [GET]>\n   330→\n   331→      >>> s = requests.Session()\n   332→      >>> s.send(r)\n   333→      <Response [200]>\n   334→    \"\"\"\n   335→\n   336→    def __init__(self):\n   337→        #: HTTP verb to send to the server.\n   338→        self.method = None\n   339→        #: HTTP URL to send the request to.\n   340→        self.url = None\n   341→        #: dictionary of HTTP headers.\n   342→        self.headers = None\n   343→        # The `CookieJar` used to create the Cookie header will be stored here\n   344→        # after prepare_cookies is called\n   345→        self._cookies = None\n   346→        #: request body to send to the server.\n   347→        self.body = None\n   348→        #: dictionary of callback hooks, for internal usage.\n   349→        self.hooks = default_hooks()\n   350→        #: integer denoting starting position of a readable file-like body.\n   351→        self._body_position = None\n   352→\n   353→    def prepare(\n   354→        self,\n   355→        method=None,\n   356→        url=None,\n   357→        headers=None,\n   358→        files=None,\n   359→        data=None,\n   360→        params=None,\n   361→        auth=None,\n   362→        cookies=None,\n   363→        hooks=None,\n   364→        json=None,\n   365→    ):\n   366→        \"\"\"Prepares the entire request with the given parameters.\"\"\"\n   367→\n   368→        self.prepare_method(method)\n   369→        self.prepare_url(url, params)\n   370→        self.prepare_headers(headers)\n   371→        self.prepare_cookies(cookies)\n   372→        self.prepare_body(data, files, json)\n   373→        self.prepare_auth(auth, url)\n   374→\n   375→        # Note that prepare_auth must be last to enable authentication schemes\n   376→        # such as OAuth to work on a fully prepared request.\n   377→\n   378→        # This MUST go after prepare_auth. Authenticators could add a hook\n   379→        self.prepare_hooks(hooks)\n   380→\n   381→    def __repr__(self):\n   382→        return f\"<PreparedRequest [{self.method}]>\"\n   383→\n   384→    def copy(self):\n   385→        p = PreparedRequest()\n   386→        p.method = self.method\n   387→        p.url = self.url\n   388→        p.headers = self.headers.copy() if self.headers is not None else None\n   389→        p._cookies = _copy_cookie_jar(self._cookies)\n   390→        p.body = self.body\n   391→        p.hooks = self.hooks\n   392→        p._body_position = self._body_position\n   393→        return p\n   394→\n   395→    def prepare_method(self, method):\n   396→        \"\"\"Prepares the given HTTP method.\"\"\"\n   397→        self.method = method\n   398→        if self.method is not None:\n   399→            self.method = to_native_string(self.method.upper())\n   400→\n   401→    @staticmethod\n   402→    def _get_idna_encoded_host(host):\n   403→        import idna\n   404→\n   405→        try:\n   406→            host = idna.encode(host, uts46=True).decode(\"utf-8\")\n   407→        except idna.IDNAError:\n   408→            raise UnicodeError\n   409→        return host\n   410→\n   411→    def prepare_url(self, url, params):\n   412→        \"\"\"Prepares the given HTTP URL.\"\"\"\n   413→        #: Accept objects that have string representations.\n   414→        #: We're unable to blindly call unicode/str functions\n   415→        #: as this will include the bytestring indicator (b'')\n   416→        #: on python 3.x.\n   417→        #: https://github.com/psf/requests/pull/2238\n   418→        if isinstance(url, bytes):\n   419→            url = url.decode(\"utf8\")\n   420→        else:\n   421→            url = str(url)\n   422→\n   423→        # Remove leading whitespaces from url\n   424→        url = url.lstrip()\n   425→\n   426→        # Don't do any URL preparation for non-HTTP schemes like `mailto`,\n   427→        # `data` etc to work around exceptions from `url_parse`, which\n   428→        # handles RFC 3986 only.\n   429→        if \":\" in url and not url.lower().startswith(\"http\"):\n   430→            self.url = url\n   431→            return\n   432→\n   433→        # Support for unicode domain names and paths.\n   434→        try:\n   435→            scheme, auth, host, port, path, query, fragment = parse_url(url)\n   436→        except LocationParseError as e:\n   437→            raise InvalidURL(*e.args)\n   438→\n   439→        if not scheme:\n   440→            raise MissingSchema(\n   441→                f\"Invalid URL {url!r}: No scheme supplied. \"\n   442→                f\"Perhaps you meant https://{url}?\"\n   443→            )\n   444→\n   445→        if not host:\n   446→            raise InvalidURL(f\"Invalid URL {url!r}: No host supplied\")\n   447→\n   448→        # In general, we want to try IDNA encoding the hostname if the string contains\n   449→        # non-ASCII characters. This allows users to automatically get the correct IDNA\n   450→        # behaviour. For strings containing only ASCII characters, we need to also verify\n   451→        # it doesn't start with a wildcard (*), before allowing the unencoded hostname.\n   452→        if not unicode_is_ascii(host):\n   453→            try:\n   454→                host = self._get_idna_encoded_host(host)\n   455→            except UnicodeError:\n   456→                raise InvalidURL(\"URL has an invalid label.\")\n   457→        elif host.startswith((\"*\", \".\")):\n   458→            raise InvalidURL(\"URL has an invalid label.\")\n   459→\n   460→        # Carefully reconstruct the network location\n   461→        netloc = auth or \"\"\n   462→        if netloc:\n   463→            netloc += \"@\"\n   464→        netloc += host\n   465→        if port:\n   466→            netloc += f\":{port}\"\n   467→\n   468→        # Bare domains aren't valid URLs.\n   469→        if not path:\n   470→            path = \"/\"\n   471→\n   472→        if isinstance(params, (str, bytes)):\n   473→            params = to_native_string(params)\n   474→\n   475→        enc_params = self._encode_params(params)\n   476→        if enc_params:\n   477→            if query:\n   478→                query = f\"{query}&{enc_params}\"\n   479→            else:\n   480→                query = enc_params\n   481→\n   482→        url = requote_uri(urlunparse([scheme, netloc, path, None, query, fragment]))\n   483→        self.url = url\n   484→\n   485→    def prepare_headers(self, headers):\n   486→        \"\"\"Prepares the given HTTP headers.\"\"\"\n   487→\n   488→        self.headers = CaseInsensitiveDict()\n   489→        if headers:\n   490→            for header in headers.items():\n   491→                # Raise exception on invalid header value.\n   492→                check_header_validity(header)\n   493→                name, value = header\n   494→                self.headers[to_native_string(name)] = value\n   495→\n   496→    def prepare_body(self, data, files, json=None):\n   497→        \"\"\"Prepares the given HTTP body data.\"\"\"\n   498→\n   499→        # Check if file, fo, generator, iterator.\n   500→        # If not, run through normal process.\n   501→\n   502→        # Nottin' on you.\n   503→        body = None\n   504→        content_type = None\n   505→\n   506→        if not data and json is not None:\n   507→            # urllib3 requires a bytes-like body. Python 2's json.dumps\n   508→            # provides this natively, but Python 3 gives a Unicode string.\n   509→            content_type = \"application/json\"\n   510→\n   511→            try:\n   512→                body = complexjson.dumps(json, allow_nan=False)\n   513→            except ValueError as ve:\n   514→                raise InvalidJSONError(ve, request=self)\n   515→\n   516→            if not isinstance(body, bytes):\n   517→                body = body.encode(\"utf-8\")\n   518→\n   519→        is_stream = all(\n   520→            [\n   521→                hasattr(data, \"__iter__\"),\n   522→                not isinstance(data, (basestring, list, tuple, Mapping)),\n   523→            ]\n   524→        )\n   525→\n   526→        if is_stream:\n   527→            try:\n   528→                length = super_len(data)\n   529→            except (TypeError, AttributeError, UnsupportedOperation):\n   530→                length = None\n   531→\n   532→            body = data\n   533→\n   534→            if getattr(body, \"tell\", None) is not None:\n   535→                # Record the current file position before reading.\n   536→                # This will allow us to rewind a file in the event\n   537→                # of a redirect.\n   538→                try:\n   539→                    self._body_position = body.tell()\n   540→                except OSError:\n   541→                    # This differentiates from None, allowing us to catch\n   542→                    # a failed `tell()` later when trying to rewind the body\n   543→                    self._body_position = object()\n   544→\n   545→            if files:\n   546→                raise NotImplementedError(\n   547→                    \"Streamed bodies and files are mutually exclusive.\"\n   548→                )\n   549→\n   550→            if length:\n   551→                self.headers[\"Content-Length\"] = builtin_str(length)\n   552→            else:\n   553→                self.headers[\"Transfer-Encoding\"] = \"chunked\"\n   554→        else:\n   555→            # Multi-part file uploads.\n   556→            if files:\n   557→                (body, content_type) = self._encode_files(files, data)\n   558→            else:\n   559→                if data:\n   560→                    body = self._encode_params(data)\n   561→                    if isinstance(data, basestring) or hasattr(data, \"read\"):\n   562→                        content_type = None\n   563→                    else:\n   564→                        content_type = \"application/x-www-form-urlencoded\"\n   565→\n   566→            self.prepare_content_length(body)\n   567→\n   568→            # Add content-type if it wasn't explicitly provided.\n   569→            if content_type and (\"content-type\" not in self.headers):\n   570→                self.headers[\"Content-Type\"] = content_type\n   571→\n   572→        self.body = body\n   573→\n   574→    def prepare_content_length(self, body):\n   575→        \"\"\"Prepare Content-Length header based on request method and body\"\"\"\n   576→        if body is not None:\n   577→            length = super_len(body)\n   578→            if length:\n   579→                # If length exists, set it. Otherwise, we fallback\n   580→                # to Transfer-Encoding: chunked.\n   581→                self.headers[\"Content-Length\"] = builtin_str(length)\n   582→        elif (\n   583→            self.method not in (\"GET\", \"HEAD\")\n   584→            and self.headers.get(\"Content-Length\") is None\n   585→        ):\n   586→            # Set Content-Length to 0 for methods that can have a body\n   587→            # but don't provide one. (i.e. not GET or HEAD)\n   588→            self.headers[\"Content-Length\"] = \"0\"\n   589→\n   590→    def prepare_auth(self, auth, url=\"\"):\n   591→        \"\"\"Prepares the given HTTP auth data.\"\"\"\n   592→\n   593→        # If no Auth is explicitly provided, extract it from the URL first.\n   594→        if auth is None:\n   595→            url_auth = get_auth_from_url(self.url)\n   596→            auth = url_auth if any(url_auth) else None\n   597→\n   598→        if auth:\n   599→            if isinstance(auth, tuple) and len(auth) == 2:\n   600→                # special-case basic HTTP auth\n   601→                auth = HTTPBasicAuth(*auth)\n   602→\n   603→            # Allow auth to make its changes.\n   604→            r = auth(self)\n   605→\n   606→            # Update self to reflect the auth changes.\n   607→            self.__dict__.update(r.__dict__)\n   608→\n   609→            # Recompute Content-Length\n   610→            self.prepare_content_length(self.body)\n   611→\n   612→    def prepare_cookies(self, cookies):\n   613→        \"\"\"Prepares the given HTTP cookie data.\n   614→\n   615→        This function eventually generates a ``Cookie`` header from the\n   616→        given cookies using cookielib. Due to cookielib's design, the header\n   617→        will not be regenerated if it already exists, meaning this function\n   618→        can only be called once for the life of the\n   619→        :class:`PreparedRequest <PreparedRequest>` object. Any subsequent calls\n   620→        to ``prepare_cookies`` will have no actual effect, unless the \"Cookie\"\n   621→        header is removed beforehand.\n   622→        \"\"\"\n   623→        if isinstance(cookies, cookielib.CookieJar):\n   624→            self._cookies = cookies\n   625→        else:\n   626→            self._cookies = cookiejar_from_dict(cookies)\n   627→\n   628→        cookie_header = get_cookie_header(self._cookies, self)\n   629→        if cookie_header is not None:\n   630→            self.headers[\"Cookie\"] = cookie_header\n   631→\n   632→    def prepare_hooks(self, hooks):\n   633→        \"\"\"Prepares the given hooks.\"\"\"\n   634→        # hooks can be passed as None to the prepare method and to this\n   635→        # method. To prevent iterating over None, simply use an empty list\n   636→        # if hooks is False-y\n   637→        hooks = hooks or []\n   638→        for event in hooks:\n   639→            self.register_hook(event, hooks[event])\n   640→\n   641→\n   642→class Response:\n   643→    \"\"\"The :class:`Response <Response>` object, which contains a\n   644→    server's response to an HTTP request.\n   645→    \"\"\"\n   646→\n   647→    __attrs__ = [\n   648→        \"_content\",\n   649→        \"status_code\",\n   650→        \"headers\",\n   651→        \"url\",\n   652→        \"history\",\n   653→        \"encoding\",\n   654→        \"reason\",\n   655→        \"cookies\",\n   656→        \"elapsed\",\n   657→        \"request\",\n   658→    ]\n   659→\n   660→    def __init__(self):\n   661→        self._content = False\n   662→        self._content_consumed = False\n   663→        self._next = None\n   664→\n   665→        #: Integer Code of responded HTTP Status, e.g. 404 or 200.\n   666→        self.status_code = None\n   667→\n   668→        #: Case-insensitive Dictionary of Response Headers.\n   669→        #: For example, ``headers['content-encoding']`` will return the\n   670→        #: value of a ``'Content-Encoding'`` response header.\n   671→        self.headers = CaseInsensitiveDict()\n   672→\n   673→        #: File-like object representation of response (for advanced usage).\n   674→        #: Use of ``raw`` requires that ``stream=True`` be set on the request.\n   675→        #: This requirement does not apply for use internally to Requests.\n   676→        self.raw = None\n   677→\n   678→        #: Final URL location of Response.\n   679→        self.url = None\n   680→\n   681→        #: Encoding to decode with when accessing r.text.\n   682→        self.encoding = None\n   683→\n   684→        #: A list of :class:`Response <Response>` objects from\n   685→        #: the history of the Request. Any redirect responses will end\n   686→        #: up here. The list is sorted from the oldest to the most recent request.\n   687→        self.history = []\n   688→\n   689→        #: Textual reason of responded HTTP Status, e.g. \"Not Found\" or \"OK\".\n   690→        self.reason = None\n   691→\n   692→        #: A CookieJar of Cookies the server sent back.\n   693→        self.cookies = cookiejar_from_dict({})\n   694→\n   695→        #: The amount of time elapsed between sending the request\n   696→        #: and the arrival of the response (as a timedelta).\n   697→        #: This property specifically measures the time taken between sending\n   698→        #: the first byte of the request and finishing parsing the headers. When stream=True is set on the\n   803→        request, this avoids reading the content at once into memory for\n   804→        large responses.  The chunk size is the number of bytes it should\n   805→        read into memory.  This is not necessarily the length of each item\n   806→        returned as decoding can take place.\n   807→\n   808→        chunk_size must be of type int or None. A value of None will\n   809→        function differently depending on the value of `stream`.\n   810→        stream=True will read data as it arrives in whatever size the\n   811→        chunks are received. If stream=False, data is returned as\n   812→        a single chunk.\n   813→\n   814→        If decode_unicode is True, content will be decoded using the best\n   815→        available encoding based on the response.\n   816→        \"\"\"\n   817→\n   818→        def generate():\n   819→            # RequestHooksMixin:\n    def register_hook(self, event, hook):\n        \"\"\"Properly register a hook.\"\"\"\n\n        if event not in self.hooks:\n            raise ValueError(f'Unsupported event specified, with event name \"{event}\"')\n\n        if isinstance(hook, Callable):\n            self.hooks[event].append(hook)\n        elif hasattr(hook, \"__iter__\"):\n            self.hooks[event].extend(h for h in hook if isinstance(h, Callable))\n\n    def deregister_hook(self, event, hook):\n        \"\"\"Deregister a previously registered hook.\n        Returns True if the hook existed, False if not.\n        \"\"\"\n\n        try:\n            self.hooks[event].remove(hook)\n            return True\n        except ValueError:\n            return False\n\n\nclass Request(RequestHooksMixin):\n    \"\"\"A user-created :class:`Request <Request>` object.\n\n    Used to prepare a :class:`PreparedRequest <PreparedRequest>`, which is sent to the server.\n\n    :param method: HTTP method to use.\n    :param url: URL to send.\n    :param headers: dictionary of headers to send.\n    :param files: dictionary of {filename: fileobject} files to multipart upload.\n    :param data: the body to attach to the request. If a dictionary or\n        list of tuples ``[(key, value)]`` is provided, form-encoding will\n        take place.\n    :param json: json for the body to attach to the request (if files or data is not specified).\n    :param params: URL parameters to append to the URL.     headers=None,\n        files=None,\n        data=None,\n        params=None,\n        auth=None,\n        cookies=None,\n        hooks=None,\n        json=None,\n    ):\n        # Default empty :class:`PreparedRequest <PreparedRequest>` for transmission and returns it.\"\"\"\n        p = PreparedRequest()\n        p.prepare(\n            method=self.method,\n            url=self.url,\n :class:`PreparedRequest <PreparedRequest>` object,\n    containing the exact bytes that will be sent to the server.\n\n    Instances are generated from a :class:`Request <Request>` object, and\n    should not be instantiated manually; doing so may produce undesirable\n    effects.\n\n    Usage::\n\n      >>> import requests\n      >>> req = requests.Request('GET', 'https://httpbin.org/get')\n      >>> r = req.prepare()\n      >>> r\n      <PreparedRequest [GET]>\n\n      >>> s = requests.Session()\n      >>> s.send(r)\n      <Response [200]>\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        #: HTTP verb to send to the server.\n        self.method = None\n        #: HTTP URL to send the request to.\n        self.url = None\n        #: dictionary of HTTP headers.\n        self.headers = None\n        # The `CookieJar` used to create the Cookie header will be stored here\n        # after prepare_cookies is called\n        self._cookies = None\n        #: request body to send to the server.\n        self.body = None\n        #: dictionary of callback hooks, for internal usage.\n        self.hooks = default_hooks()\n        #: integer denoting starting position of a readable file-like body.\n        self._body_position = None\n\n    def prepare(\n        self,\n        method=None,\n        url=None,\n        headers=None,\n        files=None,\n        data=None,\n        params=None,\n        auth=None,\n        cookies=None,\n        hooks=None,\n        json=None,\n    ):\n        \"\"\"Prepares the entire request with the given parameters.\"\"\"\n\n        self.prepare_method(method)\n        self.prepare_url(url, params)\n        self.prepare_headers(headers)\n        self.prepare_cookies(cookies)\n        self.prepare_body(data, files, json)\n        self.prepare_auth(auth, url)\n\n        # Note that prepare_auth must be last to enable authentication schemes\n        # such as OAuth to work on a fully prepared request.\n\n        # This MUST go after prepare_auth. Authenticators could add a hook\n        self.prepare_hooks(hooks)\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return f\"<PreparedRequest [{self.method}]>\"\n\n    def copy(self):\n        p = PreparedRequest()\n        p.method = self.method\n        p.url = self.url\n        p.headers = self.headers.copy() if self.headers is not None else None\n        p._cookies = _copy_cookie_jar(self._cookies)\n        p.body = self.body\n        p.hooks = self.hooks\n        p._body_position = self._body_position\n        return p\n\n    def prepare_method(self, method):\n        \"\"\"Prepares the given HTTP method.\"\"\"\n        self.method = method\n        if self.method is not None:\n            self.method = to_native_string(self.method.upper())\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _get_idna_encoded_host(host):\n        import idna\n\n        try:\n            host = idna.encode(host, uts46=True).decode(\"utf-8\")\n        except idna.IDNAError:\n            raise UnicodeError\n        return host\n\n    def prepare_url(self, url, params):\n        \"\"\"Prepares the given HTTP URL.\"\"\"\n        #: Accept objects that have string representations.\n        #: We're unable to blindly call unicode/str functions\n        #: as this will include the bytestring indicator (b'')\n        #: on python 3.x.\n        #: https://github.com/psf/requests/pull/2238\n        if isinstance(url, bytes):\n            url = url.decode(\"utf8\")\n        else:\n            url = str(url)\n\n        # Remove leading whitespaces from url\n        url = url.lstrip()\n\n        # Don't do any URL preparation for non-HTTP schemes like `mailto`,\n        # `data` etc to work around exceptions from `url_parse`, which\n        # handles RFC 3986 only.\n        if \":\" in url and not url.lower().startswith(\"http\"):\n            self.url = url\n            return\n\n        # Support for unicode domain names and paths.\n        try:\n            scheme, auth, host, port, path, query, fragment = parse_url(url)\n        except LocationParseError as e:\n            raise InvalidURL(*e.args)\n\n        if not scheme:\n            raise MissingSchema(\n                f\"Invalid URL {url!r}: No scheme supplied. \"\n                f\"Perhaps you meant https://{url}?\"\n            )\n\n        if not host:\n            raise InvalidURL(f\"Invalid URL {url!r}: No host supplied\")\n\n        # In general, we want to try IDNA encoding the hostname if the string contains\n        # non-ASCII characters. This allows users to automatically get the correct IDNA\n        # behaviour. For strings containing only ASCII characters, we need to also verify\n        # it doesn't start with a wildcard (*), before allowing the unencoded hostname.\n        if not unicode_is_ascii(host):\n            try:\n                host = self._get_idna_encoded_host(host)\n            except UnicodeError:\n                raise InvalidURL(\"URL has an invalid label.\")\n        elif host.startswith((\"*\", \".\")):\n            raise InvalidURL(\"URL has an invalid label.\")\n\n        # Carefully reconstruct the network location\n        netloc = auth or \"\"\n        if netloc:\n            netloc += \"@\"\n        netloc += host\n        if port:\n            netloc += f\":{port}\"\n\n        # Bare domains aren't valid URLs.\n        if not path:\n            path = \"/\"\n\n        if isinstance(params, (str, bytes)):\n            params = to_native_string(params)\n\n        enc_params = self._encode_params(params)\n        if enc_params:\n            if query:\n                query = f\"{query}&{enc_params}\"\n            else:\n                query = enc_params\n\n        url = requote_uri(urlunparse([scheme, netloc, path, None, query, fragment]))\n        self.url = url\n\n    def prepare_headers(self, headers):\n        \"\"\"Prepares the given HTTP headers.\"\"\"\n\n        self.headers = CaseInsensitiveDict()\n        if headers:\n            for header in headers.items():\n                # Raise exception on invalid header value.\n                check_header_validity(header)\n                name, value = header\n                self.headers[to_native_string(name)] = value\n\n    def prepare_body(self, data, files, json=None):\n        \"\"\"Prepares the given HTTP body data.\"\"\"\n\n        # Check if file, fo, generator, iterator.\n        # If not, run through normal process.\n\n        # Nottin' on you.\n        body = None\n        content_type = None\n\n        if not data and json is not None:\n            # urllib3 requires a bytes-like body. Python 2's json.dumps\n            # provides this natively, but Python 3 gives a Unicode string.\n            content_type = \"application/json\"\n\n            try:\n                body = complexjson.dumps(json, allow_nan=False)\n            except ValueError as ve:\n                raise InvalidJSONError(ve, request=self)\n\n            if not isinstance(body, bytes):\n                body = body.encode(\"utf-8\")\n\n        is_stream = all(\n            [\n                hasattr(data, \"__iter__\"),\n                not isinstance(data, (basestring, list, tuple, Mapping)),\n            ]\n        )\n\n        if is_stream:\n            try:\n                length = super_len(data)\n            except (TypeError, AttributeError, UnsupportedOperation):\n                length = None\n\n            body = data\n\n            if getattr(body, \"tell\", None) is not None:\n                # Record the current file position before reading.\n                # This will allow us to rewind a file in the event\n                # of a redirect.\n                try:\n                    self._body_position = body.tell()\n                except OSError:\n                    # This differentiates from None, allowing us to catch\n                    # a failed `tell()` later when trying to rewind the body\n                    self._body_position = object()\n\n            if files:\n                raise NotImplementedError(\n                    \"Streamed bodies and files are mutually exclusive.\"\n                )\n\n            if length:\n                self.headers[\"Content-Length\"] = builtin_str(length)\n            else:\n                self.headers[\"Transfer-Encoding\"] = \"chunked\"\n        else:\n            # Multi-part file uploads.\n            if files:\n                (body, content_type) = self._encode_files(files, data)\n            else:\n                if data:\n                    body = self._encode_params(data)\n                    if isinstance(data, basestring) or hasattr(data, \"read\"):\n                        content_type = None\n                    else:\n                        content_type = \"application/x-www-form-urlencoded\"\n\n            self.prepare_content_length(body)\n\n            # Add content-type if it wasn't explicitly provided.\n            if content_type and (\"content-type\" not in self.headers):\n                self.headers[\"Content-Type\"] = content_type\n\n        self.body = body\n\n    def prepare_content_length(self, body):\n        \"\"\"Prepare Content-Length header based on request method and body\"\"\"\n        if body is not None:\n            length = super_len(body)\n            if length:\n                # If length exists, set it. Otherwise, we fallback\n                # to Transfer-Encoding: chunked.\n                self.headers[\"Content-Length\"] = builtin_str(length)\n        elif (\n            self.method not in (\"GET\", \"HEAD\")\n            and self.headers.get(\"Content-Length\") is None\n        ):\n            # Set Content-Length to 0 for methods that can have a body\n            # but don't provide one. (i.e. not GET or HEAD)\n            self.headers[\"Content-Length\"] = \"0\"\n\n    def prepare_auth(self, auth, url=\"\"):\n        \"\"\"Prepares the given HTTP auth data.\"\"\"\n\n        # If no Auth is explicitly provided, extract it from the URL first.\n        if auth is None:\n            url_auth = get_auth_from_url(self.url)\n            auth = url_auth if any(url_auth) else None\n\n        if auth:\n            if isinstance(auth, tuple) and len(auth) == 2:\n                # special-case basic HTTP auth\n                auth = HTTPBasicAuth(*auth)\n\n            # Allow auth to make its changes.\n            r = auth(self)\n\n            # Update self to reflect the auth changes.\n            self.__dict__.update(r.__dict__)\n\n            # Recompute Content-Length\n            self.prepare_content_length(self.body)\n\n    def prepare_cookies(self, cookies):\n        \"\"\"Prepares the given HTTP cookie data.\n\n        This function eventually generates a ``Cookie`` header from the\n        given cookies using cookielib. Due to cookielib's design, the header\n        will not be regenerated if it already exists, meaning this function\n        can only be called once for the life of the\n        :class:`PreparedRequest <PreparedRequest>` object. Any subsequent calls\n        to ``prepare_cookies`` will have no actual effect, unless the \"Cookie\"\n        header is removed beforehand.\n        \"\"\"\n        if isinstance(cookies, cookielib.CookieJar):\n            self._cookies = cookies\n        else:\n            self._cookies = cookiejar_from_dict(cookies)\n\n        cookie_header = get_cookie_header(self._cookies, self)\n        if cookie_header is not None:\n            self.headers[\"Cookie\"] = cookie_header\n\n    def prepare_hooks(self, hooks):\n        \"\"\"Prepares the given hooks.\"\"\"\n        # hooks can be passed as None to the prepare method and to this\n        # method. To prevent iterating over None, simply use an empty list\n        # if hooks is False-y\n        hooks = hooks or []\n        for event in hooks:\n            self.register_hook(event, hooks[event])\n\n\nclass Response:\n    \"\"\"The :class:`Response <Response>` object, which contains a\n    server's response to an HTTP request.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    __attrs__ = [\n        \"_content\",\n        \"status_code\",\n        \"headers\",\n        \"url\",\n        \"history\",\n        \"encoding\",\n        \"reason\",\n        \"cookies\",\n        \"elapsed\",\n        \"request\",\n    ]\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self._content = False\n        self._content_consumed = False\n        self._next = None\n\n        #: Integer Code of responded HTTP Status, e.g. 404 or 200.\n        self.status_code = None\n\n        #: Case-insensitive Dictionary of Response Headers.\n        #: For example, ``headers['content-encoding']`` will return the\n        #: value of a ``'Content-Encoding'`` response header.\n        self.headers = CaseInsensitiveDict()\n\n        #: File-like object representation of response (for advanced usage).\n        #: Use of ``raw`` requires that ``stream=True`` be set on the request.\n        #: This requirement does not apply for use internally to Requests.\n        self.raw = None\n\n        #: Final URL location of Response.\n        self.url = None\n\n        #: Encoding to decode with when accessing r.text.\n        self.encoding = None\n\n        #: A list of :class:`Response <Response>` objects from\n        #: the history of the Request. Any redirect responses will end\n        #: up here. The list is sorted from the oldest to the most recent request.\n        self.history = []\n\n        #: Textual reason of responded HTTP Status, e.g. \"Not Found\" or \"OK\".\n        self.reason = None\n\n        #: A CookieJar of Cookies the server sent back.\n        self.cookies = cookiejar_from_dict({})\n\n        #: The amount of time elapsed between sending the request\n        #: and the arrival of the response (as a timedelta).\n        #: This property specifically measures the time taken between sending\n        #: the first byte of the request and finishing parsing the headers. If\n        the status code, is between 200 and 400, this will return True. This\n        is **not** a check to see if the response code is ``200 OK``.\n        \"\"\"\n        return self.ok\n\n    def __nonzero__(self):\n        \"\"\"Returns True if :attr:`status_code` is less than 400.\n\n        This attribute checks if the status code of the response is between\n        400 and 600 to see if there was a client error or a server error. If\n        the status code is between 200 and 400, this will return True. This\n        is **not** a check to see if the response code is ``200 OK``.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            self.raise_for_status()\n        except HTTPError:\n            return False\n        return True\n\n    @property\n    def is_redirect(self):\n        \"\"\"True if this Response is a well-formed HTTP redirect that could have\n        been processed automatically (by :meth:`Session.resolve_redirects`).\n        \"\"\"\n        return \"location\" in self.headers and self.status_code in REDIRECT_STATI\n\n    @property\n    def is_permanent_redirect(self):\n        \"\"\"True if this Response one of the permanent versions of redirect.\"\"\"\n        return \"location\" in self.headers and self.status_code in (\n            codes.moved_permanently,\n            codes.permanent_redirect,\n        )\n\n    @property\n    def next(self):\n        \"\"\"Returns a PreparedRequest for the next request in a redirect chain, if there is one.\"\"\"\n        return self._next\n\n    @property\n    def apparent_encoding(self):\n        \"\"\"The apparent encoding, provided by the charset_normalizer or chardet libraries.\"\"\"\n        if chardet is not None:\n            return chardet.detect(self.content)[\"encoding\"]\n        else:\n            # If no character detection library is available, we'll fall back\n            # to a standard Python utf-8 str.\n            return \"utf-8\"\n\n    def iter_content(self, chunk_size=1, decode_unicode=False):\n        \"\"\"Iterates over the response data.  When stream=True is set on the\n        request, this avoids reading the content at once into memory for\n        large responses.  The chunk size is the number of bytes it should\n        read into memory.  This is not necessarily the length of each item\n        returned as decoding can take place.\n\n        chunk_size must be of type int or None. A value of None will\n        function differently depending on the value of `stream`.\n        stream=True will read data as it arrives in whatever size the\n        chunks are received. If stream=False, data is returned as\n        a single chunk.\n\n        If decode_unicode is True, content will be decoded using the best\n        available encoding based on the response.\n        \"\"\"\n\n        def generate():\n            # Special case for urllib3.\n            if hasattr(self.raw, \"stream\"):\n                try:\n                    yield from self.raw.stream(chunk_size, decode_content=True)\n                except ProtocolError as e:\n                    raise ChunkedEncodingError(e)\n                except DecodeError as e:\n                    raise ContentDecodingError(e)\n                except ReadTimeoutError as e:\n                    raise ConnectionError(e)\n                except SSLError as e:\n                    raise RequestsSSLError(e)\n            else:\n                # Standard file-like object.\n                while True:\n                    chunk = self.raw.read(chunk_size)\n                    if not chunk:\n                        break\n                    yield chunk\n\n            self._content_consumed = True\n\n        if self._content_consumed and isinstance(self._content, bool):\n            raise StreamConsumedError()\n        elif chunk_size is not None and not isinstance(chunk_size, int):\n            raise TypeError(\n                f\"chunk_size must be an int, it is instead a {type(chunk_size)}.\"\n            )\n        # simulate reading small chunks of the content\n        reused_chunks = iter_slices(self._content, chunk_size)\n\n        stream_chunks = generate()\n\n        chunks = reused_chunks if self._content_consumed else stream_chunks\n\n        if decode_unicode:\n            chunks = stream_decode_response_unicode(chunks, self)\n\n        return chunks\n\n    def iter_lines(\n        self, chunk_size=ITER_CHUNK_SIZE, decode_unicode=False, delimiter=None\n    ):\n        \"\"\"Iterates over the response data, one line at a time.  When\n        stream=True is set on the request, this avoids reading the\n        content at once into memory for large responses.\n\n        .. note:: This method is not reentrant safe.\n        \"\"\"\n\n        pending = None\n\n        for chunk in self.iter_content(\n            chunk_size=chunk_size, decode_unicode=decode_unicode\n        ):\n            if pending is not None:\n                chunk = pending + chunk\n\n            if delimiter:\n                lines = chunk.split(delimiter)\n            else:\n                lines = chunk.splitlines()\n\n            if lines and lines[-1] and chunk and lines[-1][-1] == chunk[-1]:\n                pending = lines.pop()\n            else:\n                pending = None\n\n            yield from lines\n\n        if pending is not None:\n            yield pending\n\n    @property\n    def content(self):\n        \"\"\"Content of the response, in bytes.\"\"\"\n\n        if self._content is False:\n            # Read the contents.\n            if self._content_consumed:\n                raise RuntimeError(\"The content for this response was already consumed\")\n\n            if self.status_code == 0 or self.raw is None:\n                self._content = None\n            else:\n                self._content = b\"\".join(self.iter_content(CONTENT_CHUNK_SIZE)) or b\"\"\n\n        self._content_consumed = True\n        # don't need to release the connection; that's been handled by urllib3\n        # since we exhausted the data.\n        return self._content\n\n    @property\n    def text(self):\n        \"\"\"Content of the response, in unicode.\n\n        If Response.encoding is None, encoding will be guessed using\n        ``charset_normalizer`` or ``chardet``.\n\n        The encoding of the response content is determined based solely on HTTP\n        headers, following RFC 2616 to the letter. If you can take advantage of\n        non-HTTP knowledge to make a better guess at the encoding, you should\n        set ``r.encoding`` appropriately before accessing this property.\n        \"\"\"\n\n        # Try charset from content-type\n        content = None\n        encoding = self.encoding\n\n        if not self.content:\n            return \"\"\n\n        # Fallback to auto-detected encoding.\n        if self.encoding is None:\n            encoding = self.apparent_encoding\n\n        # Decode unicode from given encoding.\n        try:\n            content = str(self.content, encoding, errors=\"replace\")\n        except (LookupError, TypeError):\n            # A LookupError is raised if the encoding was not found which could\n            # indicate a misspelling or similar mistake.\n            #\n            # A TypeError can be raised if encoding is None\n            #\n            # So we try blindly encoding.\n            content = str(self.content, errors=\"replace\")\n\n        return content\n\n    def json(self, **kwargs):\n        r\"\"\"Decodes the JSON response body (if any) as a Python object.\n\n        This may return a dictionary, list, etc. depending on what is in the response.\n\n        :param \\*\\*kwargs: Optional arguments that ``json.loads`` takes.\n        :raises requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError: If the response body does not\n            contain valid json.\n        \"\"\"\n\n        if not self.encoding and self.content and len(self.content) > 3:\n            # No encoding set. This is an RFC violation,\n                    # and the server didn't bother to tell us what codec *was*\n                    # used.\n                    pass\n                except JSONDecodeError as e:\n                    raise RequestsJSONDecodeError(e.msg, e.doc, e.pos)\n\n        try:\n            return complexjson.loads(self.text, **kwargs)\n        except JSONDecodeError as e:\n            # Catch JSON-related errors and raise as requests.JSONDecodeError\n            # This aliases json.JSONDecodeError and simplejson.JSONDecodeError\n            raise RequestsJSONDecodeError(e.msg, e.doc, e.pos)\n\n    @property\n    def links(self):\n        \"\"\"Returns the parsed header links of the response, if any.\"\"\"\n\n        header = self.headers.get(\"link\")\n\n        resolved_links = {}\n\n        if header:\n            links = parse_header_links(header)\n\n            for link in links:\n                key = link.get(\"rel\") or link.get(\"url\")\n                resolved_links[key] = link\n\n        return resolved_links\n\n    def raise_for_status(self):\n        \"\"\"Raises :class:`HTTPError`, if one occurred.\"\"\"\n\n        http_error_msg = \"\"\n        if isinstance(self.reason, bytes):\n            # We attempt to decode utf-8 first because some servers\n            # choose to localize their reason strings. 77→\n    78→    def __repr__(self):\n    79→        if self.method is None or not self.get_full_path():\n    80→            return \"<%s>\" % self.__class__.__name__\n    81→        return \"<%s: %s def headers(self):\n    89→        return HttpHeaders(self.META)\n    90→\n    91→    @cached_property\n    92→    def accepted_types(self):\n    93→        \"\"\"\n    94→        Return a list of (\n    99→                media_type\n   100→                for token in header_value.split(\",\")\n   101→                if token.strip() and (media_type := MediaType(token)).quality != 0\n   102→            ),\n   103→            key=operator.attrgetter(\"quality\", \"specificity\"),\n   104→            reverse=True,\n   105→        )\n   106→\n   107→    @cached_property\n   108→    def accepted_types_by_precedence(self):\n   109→        \"\"\"\n   110→        Return a list of MediaType instances, in order of precedence\n   111→        (specificity).\n   112→        \"\"\"\n   113→ 119→    def accepted_type(self, media_type):\n   120→        \"\"\"\n   121→        Return the MediaType instance which best matches the given media type.\n   122→        \"\"\"\n   123→        media_type = MediaType(media_type)\n   124→        return next(\n   125→            (\n   126→                accepted_type\n   127→                for accepted_type in self.accepted_types_by_precedence\n   128→                if media_type.match(accepted_type)\n   129→            ),\n   130→            None,\n   131→        )\n   132→\n   133→    def get_preferred_type(self, media_types):\n   134→        \"\"\"Select the preferred media type from the provided options.\"\"\"\n   135→        if not media_types or not self.accepted_types:\n   136→            return None\n   137→\n   138→        desired_types = [\n   139→            (accepted_type, media_type)\n   140→            for media_type in media_types\n   141→            if (accepted_type := self.accepted_type(media_type)) is not None\n   142→        ]\n   143→\n   144→        if not desired_types:\n   145→            return None\n   146→\n   147→        # Of the desired media types, \"\"\"Does the client accept a response in the given media type?\"\"\"\n   152→        return self.accepted_type(media_type) is not None\n   153→\n   154→    def _set_content_type_params(self, meta):\n   155→        \"\"\"Set content_type, content_params, and encoding.\"\"\"\n   156→        self.content_type, self.content_params = parse_header_parameters(\n   157→ self.content_params[\"charset\"]\n   166→\n   167→    def _get_raw_host(self):\n   168→        \"\"\"\n   169→        Return the HTTP host using the environment or request headers. Skip\n   170→        allowed hosts protection, so may return an insecure host.\n   171→        \"\"\"\n   172→        # We try three options, in order of decreasing preference.\n   173→        if settings.USE_X_FORWARDED_HOST and (\"HTTP_X_FORWARDED_HOST\" in self.META):\n   174→            host = self.META[\"HTTP_X_FORWARDED_HOST\"]\n   175→        elif \"HTTP_HOST\" in self.META:\n   176→            host = self.META[\"HTTP_HOST\"]\n   177→        else:\n   178→            # Reconstruct the host using the algorithm from PEP 333.\n   179→            host = self.META[\"SERVER_NAME\"]\n   180→            server_port = self.get_port()\n   181→            if server_port != (\"443\" if self.is_secure() else \"80\"):\n   182→                host = \"%s:%s\" % (host, server_port)\n   183→        return host\n   184→\n   185→    def get_host(self):\n   186→        \"\"\"Return the HTTP host using the environment or request headers.\"\"\"\n   187→        host = self._get_raw_host()\n   188→\n   189→        # Allow variants of localhost if ALLOWED_HOSTS is empty and DEBUG=True.\n   190→        allowed_hosts = settings.ALLOWED_HOSTS\n   191→        if settings.DEBUG and not allowed_hosts:\n   192→            allowed_hosts = [\".localhost\", \"127.0.0.1\", \"[::1]\"]\n   193→\n   194→        domain, port = split_domain_port(host)\n   195→        if domain and validate_host(domain, allowed_hosts):\n   196→            return host\n   197→        else:\n   198→            msg = \"Invalid HTTP_HOST header: %r.\" % host\n   199→            if domain:\n   200→                msg += \" You may need to add %r to ALLOWED_HOSTS.\" % domain\n   201→            else:\n   202→                msg += (\n   203→                    \" The domain name provided is not valid according to RFC 1034/1035.\"\n   204→                )\n   205→            raise DisallowedHost(msg)\n   206→\n   207→    def get_port(self):\n   208→        \"\"\"Return the port number for the request as a string.\"\"\"\n   209→        if settings.USE_X_FORWARDED_PORT and \"HTTP_X_FORWARDED_PORT\" in self.META:\n   210→            port = self.META[\"HTTP_X_FORWARDED_PORT\"]\n   211→        else:\n   212→            port = self.META[\"SERVER_PORT\"]\n   213→        return str(port)\n   214→\n   215→    def get_full_path(self, force_append_slash=False):\n   216→        return self._get_full_path(self.path, force_append_slash)\n   217→\n   218→    def get_full_path_info(self, force_append_slash=False):\n   219→        return self._get_full_path(self.path_info, force_append_slash)\n   220→\n   221→    def _get_full_path(self, path, force_append_slash):\n   222→        # RFC 3986 requires query string arguments to be in the ASCII range.\n   223→        # Rather than crash if this doesn't happen, we encode defensively.\n   224→        return \"%s%s%s\" % (\n   225→            escape_uri_path(path),\n   226→            \"/\" if force_append_slash and not path.endswith(\"/\") else \"\",\n   227→            (\n   228→                (\"?\" + iri_to_uri(self.META.get(\"QUERY_STRING\", \"\")))\n   229→                if self.META.get(\"QUERY_STRING\", \"\")\n   230→                else \"\"\n   231→            ),\n   232→        )\n   233→\n   234→    def get_signed_cookie(self, key, default=RAISE_ERROR, salt=\"\", max_age=None):\n   235→        \"\"\"\n   236→        Attempt to return a signed cookie. If the signature fails or the\n   237→        cookie has expired, raise an exception, unless the `default` argument\n   238→        is provided, in which case return that value.\n   239→        \"\"\"\n   240→        try:\n   241→            cookie_value = self.COOKIES[key]\n   242→        except KeyError:\n   243→            if default is not RAISE_ERROR:\n   244→                return default\n   245→            else:\n   246→                raise\n   247→        try:\n   248→            value = signing.get_cookie_signer(salt=key + salt).unsign(\n   249→                cookie_value, max_age=max_age\n   250→            )\n   251→        except signing.BadSignature:\n   252→            if default is not RAISE_ERROR:\n   253→                return default\n   254→            else:\n   255→                raise\n   256→        return value\n   257→\n   258→    def build_absolute_uri(self, location=None):\n   259→        \"\"\"\n   260→        Build an absolute URI from the location and the variables available in\n   261→        this request. If no ``location`` is specified, build the absolute URI\n   262→        using request.get_full_path(). If the location is absolute, convert it\n   263→        to an RFC 3987 compliant URI and return it. If location is relative or\n   264→        is scheme-relative (i.e., ``//example.com/``), urljoin() it to a base\n   265→        URL constructed from the request variables.\n   266→        \"\"\"\n   267→        if location is None:\n   268→            # Make it an absolute url (but schemeless and domainless) for the\n   269→            # edge case that the path starts with '//'.\n   270→            location = \"//%s\" % self.get_full_path()\n   271→        else:\n   272→            # Coerce lazy locations.\n   273→            location = str(location)\n   274→        bits = urlsplit(location)\n   275→        if not (bits.scheme and bits.netloc):\n   276→            # Handle the simple, most common case. If the location is absolute\n   277→            # and a scheme or host (netloc) isn't provided, skip an expensive\n   278→            # urljoin() as long as no path segments are '.' or '..'.\n   279→            if (\n   280→                bits.path.startswith(\"/\")\n   281→                and not bits.scheme\n   282→                and not bits.netloc\n   283→                and \"/./\" not in bits.path\n   284→                and \"/../\" not in bits.path\n   285→            ):\n   286→                # If location starts with '//' but has no netloc, reuse the\n   287→                # schema and netloc from the current request. Strip the double\n   288→                # slashes and continue as if it wasn't specified.\n   289→                location = self._current_scheme_host + location.removeprefix(\"//\")\n   290→            else:\n   291→                # Join the constructed URL with the provided location, which\n   292→                # allows the provided location to apply query strings to the\n   293→                # base path.\n   294→                location = urljoin(self._current_scheme_host + self.path, location)\n   295→        return iri_to_uri(location)\n   296→\n   297→    @cached_property\n   298→    def _current_scheme_host(self):\n   299→        return \"{}://{}\".format(self.scheme, self.get_host())\n   300→\n   301→    def _get_scheme(self):\n   302→        \"\"\"\n   303→        Hook for subclasses like WSGIRequest to implement. QueryDict(encoding=self._encoding),\n   454→                MultiValueDict(),\n   455→            )\n   456→\n   457→    def close(self):\n   458→        if hasattr(self, \"_files\"):\n   459→            for f in chain.from_iterable(list_[1] for list_ in self._files.lists()):\n   460→                f.close()\n   461→\n   462→    # File-like and iterator interface.\n   463→    #\n   464→    # Expects self._stream to be set to an appropriate source of bytes by\n   465→    # a corresponding request subclass (e.g. WSGIRequest).\n   466→    # Also when request data has already been read by request.POST or\n   467→    # request.body, self._stream points to a BytesIO instance\n   468→    # containing that data.\n   469→\n   470→    def read(self, *args, **kwargs):\n   471→ 481→        except OSError as e:\n   482→            raise UnreadablePostError(*e.args) from e\n   483→\n   484→    def __iter__(self):\n   485→        return iter(self.readline, b\"\")\n   486→\n   487→    def readlines(self):\n   488→        return list(self)\n   489→\n   490→\n   491→class HttpHeaders(CaseInsensitiveMapping):\n   492→    HTTP_PREFIX = \"HTTP_\"\n   493→    # PEP 333 gives two headers which aren't prepended with HTTP_.\n   494→    UNPREFIXED_HEADERS = {\"CONTENT_TYPE\", \"CONTENT_LENGTH\"}\n   495→\n   496→    def __init__(self, environ):\n   497→        headers = {}\n   498→        for header, value in environ.items():\n   499→            name = self.parse_header_name(header)\n   500→            if name:\n   501→                headers[name] = value\n   502→        super().__init__(headers)\n   503→\n   504→    def __getitem__(self, key):\n   505→        \"\"\"Allow header lookup using underscores in place of hyphens.\"\"\"\n   506→        return super().__getitem__(key.replace(\"_\", \"-\"))\n   507→\n   508→    @classmethod\n   509→    def parse_header_name(cls, header):\n   510→        if header.startswith(cls.HTTP_PREFIX):\n   511→            header = header.removeprefix(cls.HTTP_PREFIX)\n   512→        elif header not in cls.UNPREFIXED_HEADERS:\n   513→            return None\n   514→        return header.replace(\"_\", \"-\").title()\n   515→\n   516→    @classmethod\n   517→    def to_wsgi_name(cls, header):\n   518→        header = header.replace(\"-\", \"_\").upper()\n   519→        if header in cls.UNPREFIXED_HEADERS:\n   520→            return header\n   521→        return f\"{cls.HTTP_PREFIX}{header}\"\n   522→\n   523→    @classmethod\n   524→    def to_asgi_name(cls, header):\n   525→        return header.replace(\"-\", \"_\").upper()\n   526→\n   527→    @classmethod\n   528→    def to_wsgi_names(cls, headers):\n   529→        return {\n   530→            cls.to_wsgi_name(header_name): value\n   531→            for header_name, value in headers.items()\n   532→        }\n   533→\n   534→    @classmethod\n   535→    def to_asgi_names(cls, headers):\n   536→        return {\n   537→            cls.to_asgi_name(header_name): value\n   538→            for header_name, value in headers.items()\n   539→        }\n   540→\n   541→\n   542→class QueryDict(MultiValueDict):\n   543→    \"\"\"\n   544→    A specialized MultiValueDict which represents a query string.\n   545→\n   546→    A QueryDict can be used to represent GET or POST data. It subclasses\n   547→    MultiValueDict since keys in such data can be repeated, for instance\n   548→    in the data from a form with a <select multiple> field.\n   549→\n   550→    By default QueryDicts are immutable, though the copy() method\n   551→    will always return a mutable copy.\n   552→\n   553→    Both keys and values set on this class are converted from the given\n   554→    encoding (DEFAULT_CHARSET by default) to str.\n   555→    \"\"\"\n   556→\n   557→    # These are both reset in __init__, but is specified here at the class\n   558→    # level so that unpickling will have valid values\n   559→    _mutable = True\n   560→    _encoding = None\n   561→\n   562→    def __init__(self, query_string=None, mutable=False, encoding=None):\n   563→        super().__init__()\n   564→        self.encoding = encoding or settings.DEFAULT_CHARSET\n   565→        query_string = query_string or \"\"\n   566→        parse_qsl_kwargs = {\n   567→            \"keep_blank_values\": True,\n   568→            \"encoding\": self.encoding,\n   569→            \"max_num_fields\": settings.DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_NUMBER_FIELDS,\n   570→        }\n   571→        if isinstance(query_string, bytes):\n   572→            # query_string normally contains URL-encoded data, a subset of\n   573→            # ASCII.\n   574→            try:\n   575→                query_string = query_string.decode(self.encoding)\n   576→            except UnicodeDecodeError:\n   577→                # ... but some user agents are misbehaving :-(\n   578→                query_string = query_string.decode(\"iso-8859-1\")\n   579→        try:\n   580→            for key, value in parse_qsl(query_string, **parse_qsl_kwargs):\n   581→                self.appendlist(key, value)\n   582→        except ValueError as e:\n   583→            # ValueError can also be raised if the strict_parsing argument to\n   584→            # parse_qsl() is True. As that is not used by Django, assume that\n   585→            # the exception was raised by exceeding the value of max_num_fields\n   586→            # instead of fragile checks of exception message strings.\n   587→            raise TooManyFieldsSent(\n   588→                \"The number of GET/POST parameters exceeded \"\n   589→                \"settings.DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_NUMBER_FIELDS.\"\n   590→            ) from e\n   591→        self._mutable = mutable\n   592→\n   593→    @classmethod\n   594→    def fromkeys(cls, iterable, value=\"\", mutable=False, encoding=None):\n   595→        \"\"\"\n   596→        Return a new QueryDict with keys (may be repeated) from an iterable and\n   597→        values from value.\n   598→        \"\"\"\n   599→        q = cls(\"\", mutable=True, encoding=encoding)\n   600→        for key in iterable:\n   601→            q.appendlist(key, value)\n   602→        if not mutable:\n   603→            q._mutable = False\n   604→        return q\n   605→\n   606→    @property\n   607→    def encoding(self):\n   608→        if self._encoding is None:\n   609→            self._encoding = settings.DEFAULT_CHARSET\n   610→        return self._encoding\n   611→\n   612→    @encoding.setter\n   613→    def encoding(self, value):\n   614→        self._encoding = value\n   615→\n   616→    def _assert_mutable(self):\n   617→        if not self._mutable:\n   618→            raise AttributeError(\"This QueryDict instance is immutable\")\n   619→\n   620→    def __setitem__(self, key, value):\n   621→        self._assert_mutable()\n   622→        key = bytes_to_text(key, self.encoding)\n   623→        value = bytes_to_text(value, self.encoding)\n   624→        super().__setitem__(key, value)\n   625→\n   626→    def __delitem__(self, key):\n   627→        self._assert_mutable()\n   628→        super().__delitem__(key)\n   629→\n   630→    def __copy__(self):\n   631→        result = self.__class__(\"\", mutable=True, encoding=self.encoding)\n   632→        for key, value in self.lists():\n   633→            result.setlist(key, value)\n   634→        return result\n   635→\n   636→    def __deepcopy__(self, memo):\n   637→        result = self.__class__(\"\", mutable=True, encoding=self.encoding)\n   638→        memo[id(self)] = result\n   639→        for key, value in self.lists():\n   640→            result.setlist(copy.deepcopy(key, memo), copy.deepcopy(value, memo))\n   641→        return result\n   642→\n   643→    def setlist(self, key, list_):\n   644→        self._assert_mutable()\n   645→        key = bytes_to_text(key, self.encoding)\n   646→        list_ = [bytes_to_text(elt, self.encoding) for elt in list_]\n   647→        super().setlist(key, Any pattern beginning with a period\n   832→    matches a domain and all its subdomains (e.g. ``.example.com`` matches\n   833→    ``example.com`` and any subdomain), ``*`` matches anything, and anything\n   834→    else must match exactly.\n   835→\n   836→    Note: This function assumes that the given host is lowercased and has\n   837→    already had the port, if any, stripped off.\n   838→\n   839→    Return ``True`` for a valid host, ``False`` otherwise.\n   840→    \"\"\"\n   841→    return any(\n   842→        pattern == \"*\" or is_same_domain(host, pattern) for pattern in allowed_hosts\n   843→    )\n   844→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. If no ``location`` is specified, build the absolute URI\n        using request.get_full_path(). If the location is absolute, convert it\n        to an RFC 3987 compliant URI and return it. If location is relative or\n        is scheme-relative (i.e., ``//example.com/``), urljoin() it to a base\n        URL constructed from the request variables.\n        \"\"\"\n        if location is None:\n            # Make it an absolute url (but schemeless and domainless) for the\n            # edge case that the path starts with '//'.\n            location = \"//%s\" % self.get_full_path()\n        else:\n            # Coerce lazy locations.\n            location = str(location)\n        bits = urlsplit(location)\n        if not (bits.scheme and bits.netloc):\n            # Handle the simple, most common case. If the location is absolute\n            # and a scheme or host (netloc) isn't provided, skip an expensive\n            # urljoin() as long as no path segments are '.' or '..'.\n            if (\n                bits.path.startswith(\"/\")\n                and not bits.scheme\n                and not bits.netloc\n                and \"/./\" not in bits.path\n                and \"/../\" not in bits.path\n            ):\n                # If location starts with '//' but has no netloc, reuse the\n                # schema and netloc from the current request. Limit the maximum request data size that will be handled\n            # in-memory.\n            if (\n                settings.DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE is not None\n                and int(self.META.get(\"CONTENT_LENGTH\") or 0)\n                > settings.DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE\n            ):\n                raise RequestDataTooBig(\n                    \"Request body exceeded settings.DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE.\"\n                )\n\n            try:\n                self._body = self.read()\n            except OSError as e:\n                raise UnreadablePostError(*e.args) from e\n            finally:\n                self._stream.close()\n            self._stream = BytesIO(self._body)\n        return self._body\n\n    def _mark_post_parse_error(self):\n        self._post = QueryDict()\n        self._files = MultiValueDict()\n\n    def _load_post_and_files(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Populate self._post and self._files if the content-type is a form type\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.method != \"POST\":\n            self._post, self._files = (\n                QueryDict(encoding=self._encoding),\n                MultiValueDict(),\n            )\n            return\n        if self._read_started and not hasattr(self, \"_body\"):\n            self._mark_post_parse_error()\n            return\n\n        if self.content_type == \"multipart/form-data\":\n            if hasattr(self, \"_body\"):\n                # Use already read data\n                data = BytesIO(self._body)\n            else:\n                data = self\n            try:\n                self._post, self._files = self.parse_file_upload(self.META, data)\n            except (MultiPartParserError, TooManyFilesSent):\n                #
